explanation: The input requirement specifies that it must be possible ('shall be allowed') to transition to Start-up Mode by pressing the power button. Thus, whenever the power button is pressed, the system should eventually enter Start-up Mode. Among the atomic propositions, 'powerButton' represents that the power button is pressed, and 'StartUpMode' that the system is in start-up mode. The translation uses the 'whenever ... eventually ...' pattern to indicate each press should result, possibly after some processing, in entering Start-up Mode. This captures the intent that pressing the power button enables (allows) entering Start-up Mode.
decision1_substring: shall be allowed by pushing the power button located on the back side of the ventilator unit

explanation: The requirement states that the Start-up Mode can be activated by pressing the power button on the ventilator. Among the atomic propositions, 'StartUpMode' signals that the mode is active and 'powerButton' indicates the power button is pressed. There are no propositions for location or turning on explicitly, but the requirement can be interpreted as: Whenever the user presses the power button, the system is allowed to transition to Start-up Mode. Thus, the property is best captured as: whenever powerButton is true (button pressed), eventually StartUpMode becomes true (system enters Start-up Mode). This ensures the functionality described in the input is modeled: pressing the power button enables the Start-up sequence.
decision1_substring: by pushing the power button located on the back side of the ventilator unit to turn it on
decision3_substring: The transition to Start-up Mode shall be allowed

explanation: The property specifies that entering Start-up Mode (represented by the atomic proposition StartUpMode) is allowed (i.e., can occur) only when the power button has been pressed (represented by atomic proposition powerButton). The input requirement emphasizes that the transition to Start-up Mode should be controlled by the action of pressing the power button. In terms of structured natural language, this is accurately captured by 'only while powerButton, StartUpMode', which means that StartUpMode can only become true during (i.e., at a time when) powerButton is true, disallowing the transition at other times. The atomic proposition 'powerButton' directly describes the required action ('the power button ... to turn it on'), while 'StartUpMode' reflects the resulting system state ('transition to Start-up Mode').
decision1_substring: transition to Start-up Mode shall be allowed by pushing the power button located on the back side of the ventilator unit to turn it on
decision3_substring: transition to Start-up Mode shall be allowed
output_structured_natural_language: only while powerButton, immediately satisfy StartUpMode

explanation: The input requirement states that pushing the power button ("powerButton") allows the transition to Start-up Mode ("StartUpMode"). Since the requirement is about allowing transition by an action, it is best interpreted such that whenever the power button is pushed, the system should enter Start-up Mode. This is directly mapped using the template 'whenever bool_exp2, _ABSTRACT_VAR2_' with bool_exp2 set to 'powerButton' and then applying 'immediately satisfy StartUpMode', reflecting that upon pressing the power button, the Start-up Mode transition should occur. Atomic proposition 'powerButton' captures 'pushing the power button', and 'StartUpMode' captures 'Start-up Mode'.

explanation: The requirement specifies that the transition to Start-up Mode is permitted by pressing the power button. In other words, whenever the power button is pressed, it is allowed to transition into Start-up Mode by turning the ventilator on. There is no prohibition about entering Start-up Mode in other ways, but it explicitly allows the transition when the power button is pushed. This is captured by expressing that whenever the powerButton is pressed, it is allowed (i.e., possible/triggered) that StartUpMode becomes true. Based on the provided atomic propositions, 'powerButton' (power button is pressed) is a trigger condition, and 'StartUpMode' (Start-up Mode active) is the effect. Therefore, the structured NL template should be: whenever <powerButton>, eventually satisfy <StartUpMode>. This reflects the permission/possibility described in the natural language requirement.
